name: expenses-table
description: Exercises the new component grammar: tabular data whose natural shape is table > table-row > table-cell with <for> rows, an exclusive category filter, and an alert-shaped empty state.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the view is src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). Replace the counter with a small expense tracker app.

Requirements:
- A ledger of expenses presented as aligned rows and columns of data: one header row with the column titles exactly "Date", "Description", "Category", and "Amount", then one row per expense with its date, description, category, and formatted amount lined up under those titles.
- Every expense belongs to exactly one category: Food, Travel, or Office. Seed the ledger with at least five expenses spread across Food and Travel and none in Office; two of the seeds must be exactly: 2026-06-14 / "Team lunch" / Food / $42.50, and 2026-06-18 / "Flight to NYC" / Travel / $312.00.
- A category filter with options named exactly "All", "Food", "Travel", and "Office". Exactly one option is active at a time and is visually distinct; it starts on "All"; choosing an option restricts the ledger rows to that category.
- When the active filter matches no expenses (Office, at first), show in place of the rows a distinct callout titled exactly "No expenses" with the body text exactly "Nothing recorded in this category yet.".
- A status bar summarizing the currently visible expenses as exactly "<count> expenses · $<sum> total" (for example "5 expenses · $402.75 total"), updating live as the filter changes.
- Amounts are stored as integer cents in the model; all dollar formatting ("$42.50", "$312.00") happens in Zig and must be correct for any amount.
- Keep all logic in src/main.zig (Model, Msg, update); the markup only binds values and dispatches messages.
- Validate the markup with `native markup check src/app.native`, and keep `zig build test` green — extend src/tests.zig so the tests switch categories through the real dispatch path (including to the empty one) and assert the visible rows and the summary count/total.

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes and summarize what you built.

checks.description: expense rows render through a <for> loop
the empty state uses an alert/dialog-shaped surface
live snapshot shows table semantics, seeded rows, the filters, and the summary

criteria: Model/Msg/update follow The Elm Architecture idiomatically: the filter is a single enum in the model, switching it is one message, amounts live as integer cents, and no derivable state (visible rows, totals, formatted strings) is stored in the model.

Table semantics are used properly: the ledger is a real table with a header row and one row per expense, cells are text leaves, the empty state swaps the rows via markup conditionals rather than hacks, and the filtered rows come from a derived model function.
Tests are meaningful: they switch categories through the real dispatch path (including the empty one) and assert visible row contents and the count/total summary, not merely that the code compiles.
The markup stays declarative: rows render via <for>, dollar and summary formatting happens in Zig model functions, and no logic, counting, or formatting is smuggled into the view.
checks.description: quality of the expense tracker beyond compliance
